title: Add cross-session memory recall via wiki page persistence
description:
## Problem
The agent has no mechanism to persist notable discoveries or facts between play sessions. When the bot restarts, all chat-learned knowledge (locations, passwords, player preferences) is lost.

## Agent Request
From session log: "ability to remember more context in memory" and "memory recall for past session context" and "remember/save commands".

## Scope
- Add a `RememberFact` / `RecallFact` chat command that persists facts to the MemorySmith wiki via `IMemoryGateway`
- Facts stored as structured memory records with: key, value, position, timestamp, session ID
- On startup, the agent can recall relevant facts into its chat context
- LLM prompt updated to explain the remember/recall capability
- Deterministic fast-path for short recall commands

## Examples
- Player: "Remember the password is taco" → agent stores fact: {key:"password", value:"taco"}
- Player: "The diamond vein is at (-200, 12, 300)" → agent stores fact: {key:"diamond_vein_location", value:"(-200,12,300)", position:Position}
- Player: "Recall the password" → agent retrieves and responds with the stored value
- Cross-session: bot restarts, player asks "Where is the base?" → agent recalls from persisted memory

## Implementation sketch
1. Define `StoredFact` model (key, value, position?, tags, timestamp, sessionId)
2. Add `RememberTool` and `RecallTool` in `Agent.Tools`
3. Store/retrieve via `RestMemoryGateway` with search
4. On startup, load recent facts into `ChatHistory` context

## Complexity
Low-Medium. Mostly integrates existing MemoryGateway with new tool wrappers.

## Priority
Medium — addresses the most common user complaint about agent forgetfulness.
